data.title: S&T Bancorp, Inc. reports Q2 2025 net income of $31.9M, 1.32% ROA, and 8.91% ROE. Total assets $9.8B. Nonperforming assets $21.3M.
data.description: S&T Bancorp, Inc. reported a net income of $31.9 million for Q2 2025, compared to $33.4 million in Q1 2025 and $34.4 million in Q2 2024. Key figures include return on average assets of 1.32%, return on average equity of 8.91%, and net interest income growth of $3.3 million. Their total assets were $9.8 billion, with total deposits increasing by $28.0 million. The provision for credit losses was $2.0 million in Q2 2025, and nonperforming assets decreased to $21.3 million. S&T Bancorp maintains strong capital ratios, and key financial data is detailed in the article for investors to consider. This information reveals the bank's performance, growth, and stability in the market.

data.title: Cryptocurrency market down, altcoins lagging behind Bitcoin. Prices: Bitcoin $118,742.21, Ethereum $3,648.82, XRP $3.13, Dogecoin $0.2374. Trader data: 310,509 traders liquidated, $956.46 million lost in 24 hours. Trader Notes: Bitcoin in week three of uptrend phase.
data.description: The cryptocurrency market experienced a decline on Thursday, with altcoins performing worse than Bitcoin following a period of strength. Prices for various cryptocurrencies are as follows: Bitcoin at $118,742.21, Ethereum at $3,648.82, Solana at $185.82, XRP at $3.13, Dogecoin at $0.2374, and Shiba Inu at $0.00001367. Significant data reveals a 13.4% decrease in large transactions for Bitcoin and a 5.2% increase for Ethereum. Daily active addresses saw a 6.2% growth for Bitcoin but dropped by 4.8% for Ethereum. The past 24 hours saw 310,509 traders liquidated, resulting in a total of $956.46 million losses. Notably, there were net outflows of $85.96 million from spot Bitcoin ETFs, while spot Ethereum ETFs witnessed net inflows of $332.2 million according to SoSoValue data. Rekt Capital analysis indicates that Bitcoin is currently in week three of its price discovery uptrend two, a phase historically characterized by continued upward momentum. For more details, refer to the complete article on Benzinga.com.

data.title: DocGo Inc. (Nasdaq: DCGO) to release Q2 2025 financial results on August 7, 2025. Conference call at 5pm ET.
data.description: In New York today, DocGo Inc. (Nasdaq: DCGO), a prominent provider of technology-driven mobile health and medical transportation services, revealed plans to disclose its financial performance for the second quarter concluded on June 30, 2025, post-market closure on Thursday, August 7, 2025. As part of this, management is set to conduct a conference call at 5:00 p.m. ET on the same day to delve into these results. For those who wish to participate, the conference call and webcast will take place on Thursday, August 7, 2025 at 5:00 p.m. ET.

data.title: MeMed develops new test for bacterial/viral infections; differentiates in 15 mins with finger prick blood drops.
data.description: In Andover, Massachusetts, and Haifa, Israel, a new test called MeMed BV Flex has been developed to distinguish between bacterial and viral infections within just 15 minutes. This innovative test requires only a small amount of capillary blood, collected through a finger prick. The test, known as #ADLM, aims to provide rapid and accurate results to help in identifying the type of infection present.

data.title: IMAX Corp (NYSE: IMAX) reports strong Q2 2025 results with record box office in North America and global network growth.
data.description: IMAX Corporation (NYSE: IMAX) announced robust financial performance in the second quarter of 2025, showcasing the strength of its distinctive worldwide entertainment platform and diverse content offerings. The company witnessed exceptional results during this period, with significant network expansion globally, setting new box office records in North America, and achieving notable market share increases attributed to an increase in film releases utilizing IMAX technology.

data.title: Doug Shepler, a financial advisor with $110M in assets, leaves LPL for Cetera seeking efficiency and personalized service.
data.description: Financial advisor Doug Shepler, managing $110 million in assets, has recently transitioned from LPL Financial to Cetera. This move was made to join Summit Financial Networks, a distinctive community under Cetera. Cetera expressed its excitement in welcoming the veteran advisor to their platform. The transition signifies a significant shift in Shepler's career, signifying a bright future ahead with Cetera.

data.title: Dow Inc. cuts dividend by half, reports $801 million net loss with 7% revenue decline to $10.104 billion in Q2.
data.description: Dow Inc. (NYSE: DOW) reported a significant loss in the second quarter, leading to a sharp drop in its share value. The company's adjusted loss per share was 42 cents, much wider than the anticipated 12 cents. Revenue also fell by 7% year over year to $10.104 billion, missing the estimated $10.252 billion. The company posted a GAAP net loss of $801 million for the quarter, and operating EBIT swung to a loss of $21 million. Dow also announced a 50% cut in its dividend due to macroeconomic pressures, margin compression, and global trade disruptions. The company's operating cash flow from continuing operations was negative $470 million, a significant decline from the previous year. Sales dropped across all operating segments, with Packaging & Specialty Plastics revenue falling by 9% and Industrial Intermediates & Infrastructure revenue declining by 6%. Dow also cut 800 jobs and closed major European facilities to boost profitability.

data.title: Broadband equipment spending to reach $20.1B by 2028 with focus on fiber deployments and AI applications.
data.description: The emphasis on throughput, latency, and jitter has increased due to the growing implementation of AI applications and use cases. A recent report from Dell'Oro Group, known for providing market insights on telecommunications, security, and networks, highlights this trend. The report, published on July 24, 2025, in Redwood City, California, underscores the importance of optimizing performance in AI technologies. This shift in focus comes as companies recognize the significance of enhancing efficiency in handling data.

data.title: OSR Holdings to acquire Woori IO, a noninvasive glucose monitoring technology pioneer, signing Term Sheet.
data.description: A global healthcare company, OSR Holdings, Inc. (NASDAQ: OSRH), based in Bellevue, Washington, and Seoul, South Korea, revealed on July 24, 2025, that it has entered a term sheet agreement with Woori IO Co., Ltd. ("WORIO"), a company based in South Korea. OSR Holdings focuses on advancing biomedical and wellness innovation. This partnership marks a significant step in their collaboration efforts in the healthcare sector.

data.title: Elevate Entertainment introduces "$70K Side Hustle" turning weekend DJ gigs into full-time careers, celebrating special moments.
data.description: In July 24, 2025, Elevate Entertainment unveiled a professional development initiative in Springfield, Mo., and Kansas City, Mo., with remarkable success. This program has elevated part-time weekend DJ gigs into prosperous full-time occupations for entertainment experts. The initiative has positively impacted numerous professionals in the industry, leading to significant career transformations and opportunities.

data.title: Artisight debuts Voice-Activated Interpreters on Smart Hospital Platform for seamless multilingual communication between clinicians and patients in hundreds of languages.
data.description: In Chicago on July 24, 2025, Artisight introduced Voice-Activated Interpreters on its Smart Hospital Platform, allowing clinicians to communicate with patients in multiple languages. The technology provides instant access to interpretation services in hundreds of languages, enhancing communication between healthcare providers and patients. This new feature ensures constant availability of interpreters, facilitating efficient and effective patient care.

data.title: Endo presents data on plantar fibromatosis and plantar fasciitis at APMA Annual Meeting July 24-27, 2025. #podiatry #news
data.description: Endo, Inc. based in Malvern, Pennsylvania disclosed today that they will be presenting three topics concerning plantar fibromatosis (PFI) and plantar fasciitis (PFA) during the American Podiatric Medical Association Annual Meeting from July 24 to 27, 2025. The company's stock is traded on OTCQX under the ticker symbol NDOI.

data.title: S&T Bancorp, Inc. reported $31.9 million net income in Q2 2025, down from $33.4 million compared to previous year.
data.description: In Indiana, Pennsylvania on July 24, 2025, S&T Bancorp, Inc. (S&T) disclosed their financial results. The holding company for S&T Bank reported a net income of $31.9 million, translating to $0.83 per diluted share for the second quarter of 2025. In comparison, in the same period last year, they had an income of $33.4 million, with $0.87 per diluted share.
